prompt: A connection supervisor started behaving oddly during deterministic rolling drains. A small subset of transports still open when the coordinator had already cancelled the operation before connect was called. In a separate but correlated handover trace, an old transport completed a delayed receive after its replacement was online; the late payload reached the consumer and the replacement then appeared offline even though its own receive was still pending. Ordinary cancellation after connect works, and an orderly EOF from the current transport produces the expected offline event. Adapter identifiers are unique, listener counts remain flat, and the transport contract explicitly permits an outstanding receive to settle after a replacement begins. Preserve the public APIs and cancellation reason identity, do not serialize handovers or add timers, and keep the repair within the candidate files.
